---
title: "CoPE_TarPam_analysis"
author: "Colin G. Finlay"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

# Setup

```{r Setup}
# Clear environment
rm(list=ls())

#use to set working directory 
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir="~/GitHub/FlowThrough_CoastalAg/analyses")
```

```{r Load packages}
require("tidyverse")
require("lubridate")
require("ggtext")
require("ggpubr")
require("patchwork")
```

```{r Load data}
# Coastlines and People sampling sites, water quality
CoPE_data <- read.csv("../data/CoPE/NewestCOPESHEETJan2022-FEB2025Updated.csv")

# Drought.gov historical U.S. Drought Monitor
# https://www.drought.gov/historical-information?dataset=0&selectedDateUSDM=20260707&state=North-Carolina
Drought <- read.csv("../data/Drought_gov/USDM-North-Carolina.csv")
```

# Wrangle

```{r Select two or three sites of interest}
# Sites of interest
CoPE_filt <- CoPE_data |> 
  dplyr::filter(site_name %in% c("Town Commons", "Yankee Hall Rd", "Washington Bus", "Washington Waterfront"))
```

```{r wrangle specific conductivity over time}
# 1. Wrangle the data
CoPE_clean <- CoPE_filt |>
  mutate(
    # mdy() reads strings in Month/Day/Year format and converts them to Date objects
    field_date = mdy(field_date),
    
    # as.numeric() forces the character numbers into actual mathematical values
    spec_cond = as.numeric(spec_cond)
  ) |>
  # It is good practice to drop rows where spec_cond failed to convert (e.g., if a cell said "N/A")
  filter(!is.na(spec_cond), !is.na(field_date))
```

```{r Wrangle Drought.gov data}
# 1. Determine the date range from CoPE_filt
# We use mdy() here because CoPE_filt$field_date is "Month/Day/Year"
cope_dates <- mdy(CoPE_filt$field_date)
start_date <- min(cope_dates, na.rm = TRUE)
end_date <- max(cope_dates, na.rm = TRUE)

# 2. Convert and trim the Drought data
Drought_trimmed <- Drought |>
  mutate(
    # Convert "YYYY-MM-DD" character strings into Date objects
    ValidStart = ymd(ValidStart)
  ) |>
  # Filter to keep only the rows that fall within the CoPE date range
  filter(ValidStart >= start_date & ValidStart <= end_date)
```

# Plot

```{r Plot specific conductance over time}
plot.sc <- ggplot(data = CoPE_clean, aes(x = field_date, y = spec_cond, color = site_name)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) + 
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "Town Commons"          = "#E69F00", # Orange
      "Yankee Hall Rd"        = "#56B4E9", # Sky Blue
      "Washington Bus"        = "#009E73", # Bluish Green
      "Washington Waterfront" = "#CC79A7"  # Reddish Purple
    ),
    labels = c(
      "Town Commons"          = "Town Commons",
      "Yankee Hall Rd"        = "Yankee Hall Rd",
      "Washington Bus"        = "Washington Bus. 17 Bridge", 
      "Washington Waterfront" = "Washington Waterfront"
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "",
    y = "Specific Conductance (&mu;S&nbsp;cm<sup>-1</sup>)",
    color = "Site"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.title.y = element_markdown(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black") 
  )

# View the plot
plot.sc
```

```{r Plot Ammonia over time}
plot.NH4 <- ggplot(data = CoPE_clean, aes(x = field_date, y = Ammonia..mg.L., color = site_name)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) + 
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "Town Commons"          = "#E69F00", # Orange
      "Yankee Hall Rd"        = "#56B4E9", # Sky Blue
      "Washington Bus"        = "#009E73", # Bluish Green
      "Washington Waterfront" = "#CC79A7"  # Reddish Purple
    ),
    labels = c(
      "Town Commons"          = "Town Commons",
      "Yankee Hall Rd"        = "Yankee Hall Rd",
      "Washington Bus"        = "Washington Bus. 17 Bridge", 
      "Washington Waterfront" = "Washington Waterfront"
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "",
    y = "Ammonia (mg L<sup>-1</sup>)",
    color = "Site"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.title.y = element_markdown(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black") 
  )

# View the plot
plot.NH4
```

```{r Plot phosphate over time}
plot.PO4 <- ggplot(data = CoPE_clean, aes(x = field_date, y = Phosphate..mg.L., color = site_name)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) + 
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "Town Commons"          = "#E69F00", # Orange
      "Yankee Hall Rd"        = "#56B4E9", # Sky Blue
      "Washington Bus"        = "#009E73", # Bluish Green
      "Washington Waterfront" = "#CC79A7"  # Reddish Purple
    ),
    labels = c(
      "Town Commons"          = "Town Commons",
      "Yankee Hall Rd"        = "Yankee Hall Rd",
      "Washington Bus"        = "Washington Bus. 17 Bridge", 
      "Washington Waterfront" = "Washington Waterfront"
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "",
    y = "Phosphate (mg L<sup>-1</sup>)",
    color = "Site"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.title.y = element_markdown(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black") 
  )

# View the plot
plot.PO4
```

```{r Plot NOx over time}
plot.NOx <- ggplot(data = CoPE_clean, aes(x = field_date, y = Nitrate.Nitrite..mg.L., color = site_name)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) + 
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "Town Commons"          = "#E69F00", # Orange
      "Yankee Hall Rd"        = "#56B4E9", # Sky Blue
      "Washington Bus"        = "#009E73", # Bluish Green
      "Washington Waterfront" = "#CC79A7"  # Reddish Purple
    ),
    labels = c(
      "Town Commons"          = "Town Commons",
      "Yankee Hall Rd"        = "Yankee Hall Rd",
      "Washington Bus"        = "Washington Bus. 17 Bridge", 
      "Washington Waterfront" = "Washington Waterfront"
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "",
    y = "Nitrate+Nitrite (mg L<sup>-1</sup>)",
    color = "Site"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.title.y = element_markdown(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black") 
  )

# View the plot
plot.NOx
```

```{r Plot TDKN over time}
plot.TDKN <- ggplot(data = CoPE_clean, aes(x = field_date, y = Total.Dissolved.Kjeldahl.Nitrogen..mg.L., color = site_name)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) + 
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "Town Commons"          = "#E69F00", # Orange
      "Yankee Hall Rd"        = "#56B4E9", # Sky Blue
      "Washington Bus"        = "#009E73", # Bluish Green
      "Washington Waterfront" = "#CC79A7"  # Reddish Purple
    ),
    labels = c(
      "Town Commons"          = "Town Commons",
      "Yankee Hall Rd"        = "Yankee Hall Rd",
      "Washington Bus"        = "Washington Bus. 17 Bridge", 
      "Washington Waterfront" = "Washington Waterfront"
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "",
    y = "Total Dissolved Kjeldahl N (mg L<sup>-1</sup>)",
    color = "Site"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.title.y = element_markdown(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black") 
  )

# View the plot
plot.TDKN
```

```{r Plot TDKP over time}
plot.TDKP <- ggplot(data = CoPE_clean, aes(x = field_date, y = Total.Dissolved.Kjeldahl.Phosphorus..mg.L., color = site_name)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) + 
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "Town Commons"          = "#E69F00", # Orange
      "Yankee Hall Rd"        = "#56B4E9", # Sky Blue
      "Washington Bus"        = "#009E73", # Bluish Green
      "Washington Waterfront" = "#CC79A7"  # Reddish Purple
    ),
    labels = c(
      "Town Commons"          = "Town Commons",
      "Yankee Hall Rd"        = "Yankee Hall Rd",
      "Washington Bus"        = "Washington Bus. 17 Bridge", 
      "Washington Waterfront" = "Washington Waterfront"
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "",
    y = "Total Dissolved Kjeldahl P (mg L<sup>-1</sup>)",
    color = "Site"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.title.y = element_markdown(),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black") 
  )

# View the plot
plot.TDKP
```

```{r Drought plot}
# 1. Reshape the data for ggplot2
Drought_long <- Drought_trimmed |>
  # pivot_longer moves the D0-D4 columns into two columns: Category and Percent
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(D0, D1, D2, D3, D4),
    names_to = "Drought_Category",
    values_to = "Percent_Area"
  ) |>
  # Turn the category into a factor and set the levels. 
  # This tells ggplot2 the exact order to draw the layers (D0 first, D4 last).
  mutate(
    Drought_Category = factor(Drought_Category, levels = c("D0", "D1", "D2", "D3", "D4"))
  )

# 2. Create the overlapping area plot
plot.drought <- ggplot(data = Drought_long, aes(x = ValidStart, y = Percent_Area, fill = Drought_Category)) +
  # position = "identity" tells ggplot NOT to stack the values, but to overlap them
  geom_area(position = "identity") +
  # Apply the official USDM colors and rename the legend labels
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "D0" = "#FFFF00", # Yellow
      "D1" = "#FCD37F", # Tan
      "D2" = "#FFAA00", # Orange
      "D3" = "#E60000", # Red
      "D4" = "#730000"  # Dark Red
    ),
    labels = c(
      "D0" = "D0 Abnormally Dry",
      "D1" = "D1 Moderate Drought",
      "D2" = "D2 Severe Drought",
      "D3" = "D3 Extreme Drought",
      "D4" = "D4 Exceptional Drought"
    )
  ) +
  # Format the y-axis to match the 0% to 100% scale in the image
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = seq(0, 100, by = 10), 
    labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%"), 
    expand = c(0, 0), # Removes the empty padding below 0 and above 100
    limits = c(0, 100)
  ) +
  labs(
    x = NULL, # The original image doesn't have an x-axis title
    y = "Total Percent Land Area",
    fill = NULL 
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black")
  )

# View plot
plot.drought
```

## Multi-panel plots

```{r Specific conductance over drought, patchwork}
# The / symbol means "stack vertically".
Plot.SC_over_Drought.pw <- plot.sc / plot.drought

# View the plot
Plot.SC_over_Drought.pw

#ggsave("../figures/TarPam_WQ_and_drought/SC_over_Drought.png", plot=Plot.SC_over_Drought.pw, device="png", path=NULL, scale=1, width=18.5, height=15, units = "cm", dpi=600, limitsize=TRUE, bg="white")
```

```{r N concentrations}
Plot.N_3panel <- ggarrange(plot.NH4, plot.NOx, plot.TDKN, ncol = 1, nrow = 3, align = "hv", common.legend = TRUE, legend = "bottom")

Plot.N_3panel

#ggsave("../figures/TarPam_WQ_and_drought/N_3_panel.png", plot=Plot.N_3panel, device="png", path=NULL, scale=1, width=18.5, height=30, units = "cm", dpi=600, limitsize=TRUE, bg="white")
```


# End of Document

```{r session info}
# Reproducibility
sessioninfo::session_info()
```
